steps.title: Use perpendicular slopes
steps.content: A radius drawn to a tangent point is perpendicular to the tangent line. The tangent slope is $\frac{5}{4}$, so the radius from the center $(2,5)$ to $(a,-4)$ has slope $-\frac{4}{5}$.

steps.title: Write the slope equation
steps.content: Use the two points on the radius: $$(2,5) \text{ and } (a,-4).$$ The slope is $$\frac{-4-5}{a-2}=\frac{-9}{a-2}.$$ Set this equal to $-\frac{4}{5}$: $$\frac{-9}{a-2}=-\frac{4}{5}.$$

steps.title: Check the perpendicular slopes
steps.content: The radius slope is $-\frac{4}{5}$ and the tangent slope is $\frac{5}{4}$. Their product is $$-\frac{4}{5}\cdot\frac{5}{4}=-1,$$ so they are perpendicular. $\frac{53}{4}$ is correct.
